Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Mixed-Precision Training
Short Hypothesis: A curvature-aware precision routing framework can dynamically allocate precision levels to different layers of a neural network during training, optimizing both computational efficiency and model accuracy.
Related Work: Existing methods in mixed-precision training either apply uniform precision across all layers or use heuristic-based approaches that lack theoretical grounding. Recent works like SNIP and EMQ focus on adaptive precision allocation but primarily for large language models and post-training quantization, respectively. Our proposal extends these ideas by integrating curvature diagnostics and deterministic spectral estimators to provide a training-free, curvature-aware precision routing mechanism.
Abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a novel framework for mixed-precision training that dynamically adjusts the precision levels of neural network layers based on curvature diagnostics. CAPR leverages deterministic spectral estimators to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, enabling a training-free adaptive routing protocol. By isolating algorithmic pathologies from infrastructural noise, CAPR ensures numerical stability and strict computational overhead bounds. Our experimental validation on a variety of models and datasets demonstrates significant improvements in both efficiency and accuracy compared to existing methods.
Experiments: Evaluate CAPR on a range of neural network architectures (e.g., ResNet, Transformer) and datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet).
Measure the computational efficiency (e.g., FLOPs, memory usage) and model accuracy (e.g., top-1 accuracy) of CAPR compared to uniform precision and heuristic-based methods.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature diagnostics, spectral estimators, and precision routing on model performance.
Perform multi-seed variance quantification to ensure the robustness of the results.
Validate the theoretical bounds on numerical stability and computational overhead using PAC-Bayes generalization bounds and explicit condition number constraints.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of curvature diagnostics and spectral estimators may introduce additional computational overhead, which needs to be carefully managed.
The effectiveness of CAPR may vary across different architectures and datasets, requiring extensive validation.
The training-free nature of CAPR might limit its applicability in scenarios where dynamic adjustments are required during training.
